div.div: · wire puzzle in Gordian Knot configuration

Study the wire structure before making any move — rushing creates new tangles. Wire disentanglement puzzles have one correct path. Identify which loops can physically pass through which gaps, and trace the continuous wire from end to end. Work methodically from the most mobile end inward. Never force anything — if a move needs force, it's wrong. Take it one loop at a time. Wire disentanglement puzzles are still widely sold in puzzle and game shops, in dozens of designs.

Wire puzzle disentanglement became one of the most popular puzzle categories in the Victorian era, with thousands of designs sold. They were called 'patience puzzles' because rushing invariably made them worse — a useful life lesson packaged as entertainment.

A complex wire configuration forms a seemingly insoluble tangle. The puzzle is to separate the interlocking wire elements without cutting or bending them. Unlike the mythological Gordian Knot, this one has an elegant solution — but it requires understanding the topology of the wire loops.

svg.text: Source: Puzzles Old and New (1893) · Heritage Parlor
